putzke-holm.name: Holm Putzke
putzke-holm.role: Professor for Strafrecht, University Passau; Stadtrat (fraktionslos, CSU member)
putzke-holm.bio: 52-year-old law professor and criminal defense lawyer, CSU member running for FDP. Leading in polls with 42% favorability. Known for sharp rhetoric and criticism of CSU internal processes. Born 1973 in Dohna (Saxony).

rother-andreas.name: Andreas Rother
rother-andreas.bio: 51-year-old optician master and second mayor since 2020. Chosen successor of OB Jürgen Dupper. Born July 28, 1974 in Passau. Owns Akustik- & Brillenhaus Rother. Represents continuity of 18-year Dupper era.

dickl-armin.name: Armin Dickl
dickl-armin.bio: 43-year-old business consultant (Benefits sector) and third mayor since 2020. Married, twin sons (8). Nominated May 2025 with 98.5% approval. Represents historical CSU/SPD rotation pattern (CSU last held office 2002-2008).

auer-stefanie.name: Stefanie Auer
auer-stefanie.bio: Lawyer, city council faction leader for Greens, district councilor. Running on slogan 'Passau kann mehr' (Passau can do more). First woman OB candidate in Passau history. Focus on transparency, climate action, and participatory politics.
weaknesses: Green party ceiling in conservative region

kapfer-siegfried.name: Siegfried Kapfer
kapfer-siegfried.bio: Turns 70 on election day (March 8, 2026). Long-time FWG chairman and faction leader since 2014. Emphasizes local non-partisan politics and experience. Known for sports involvement (30 years as Kampfrichterobmann).

stichwahl_prediction.reason: Poll shows Putzke at 42% but unlikely to reach 50%+ in first round with 8 candidates. Traditional CSU/SPD voters likely split. Runoff between poll leader Putzke and SPD continuity candidate Rother most probable.

news.title: Putzke leads polls with 42%, Rother at 23%, Dickl at 14%
news.body: Online survey by Passauer Neue Presse shows FDP candidate Holm Putzke leading with 42 percent favorability, SPD candidate Andreas Rother at 23 percent, and CSU candidate Armin Dickl at 14 percent. Auer receives 8 percent, while Kapfer, Mangold, Vilsmeier and Kolitz each poll at 3 percent.

news.title: Jürgen Dupper steps down after 18 years
news.body: Incumbent OB Jürgen Dupper (SPD, 64) announces he will not seek re-election in March 2026 after three terms and 18 years in office. Endorses second mayor Andreas Rother as successor. Dupper held office since May 2008.

news.title: CSU member Putzke runs for FDP, criticizes party
news.body: Holm Putzke, long-time CSU member and fraktionslos city councilor, explains why he's running as FDP candidate. Criticizes CSU for promoting mediocrity and blocking his OB candidacy within party. Putzke remains CSU member but campaigns for liberal alternative.

news.title: CSU nominates Dickl with 98.5% approval
news.body: Passau CSU nominates third mayor Armin Dickl as OB candidate with overwhelming 98.5 percent support. The 42-year-old business consultant wants to bring fresh energy to city hall. CSU hopes to reclaim mayor's office after 18 years SPD rule.

news.title: SPD unanimously nominates Andreas Rother
news.body: One week after Dupper's announcement, SPD unanimously nominates second mayor Andreas Rother as OB candidate. Rother, 51, master optician and local business owner, promises continuity and evolution. Emphasizes listening to citizens and transparent governance.

news.title: Stefanie Auer first woman OB candidate in Passau
news.body: Greens nominate faction leader Stefanie Auer with full support as OB candidate. Lawyer and district councilor runs on slogan Passau kann mehr. Advocates for climate action, transparent processes, and politics at eye level. First woman to run for mayor in Passau history.

news.title: Hochwasserschutz divides candidates
news.body: Flood protection plans for Donaulände spark controversy in OB race. Vilsmeier demands end to concrete wall plans. Putzke advocates low-threshold solutions like de-sealing. Rother says wait for results. Kolitz warns against walling in the city. Issue becomes key campaign theme.

news.title: Eight candidates approved for ballot
news.body: Election committee validates eight OB candidates and party lists for city council. Besides favorites Putzke, Rother and Dickl, also running: Auer (Grüne), Kapfer (FWG), Mangold (ÖDP), Kolitz (Linke), Vilsmeier (Passauer Liste). Wahltermin March 8, 2026.

news.title: Podiumsdiskussion highlights policy divides
news.body: All eight OB candidates debate in public forum. Key divides: flood protection (concrete wall vs. alternative solutions), traffic policy, affordable housing. Putzke emphasizes fresh start, Rother continuity, Dickl economic competence. Candidates polite but clear differences visible.

news.title: CSU-SPD rotation pattern at stake
news.body: Since 1984, Passau alternated between CSU and SPD mayors. Pattern: CSU one term, SPD gets re-elected. Dupper held three terms (18 years), breaking recent trend. CSU argues it's their turn. SPD warns against mathematical entitlement. FDP's Putzke could break 40-year duopoly.

news.title: FWG nominates Siegfried Kapfer
news.body: Free voters nominate faction leader Siegfried Kapfer as OB candidate. Kapfer, who turns 70 on election day, emphasizes local roots and non-partisan pragmatism. FWG positions itself as independent alternative to traditional party politics.

news.title: ÖDP sends Urban Mangold into race again
news.body: ÖDP nominates district councilor Urban Mangold (63) as OB candidate with 30 yes votes. Mangold, former second mayor (2008-2014), ran 2014 and got 8.98%. Campaigns on ecological renewal and protecting Passau forests. ÖDP/Aktive Passauer joint list.

news.title: Linke nominates Nika Kolitz
news.body: Die Linke unanimously nominates district chairwoman Nika Kolitz as OB candidate. Lawyer and activist focuses on affordable housing, social justice, anti-racism. First Linke OB candidate in Passau. Campaigns against rent speculation and for stronger social infrastructure.

news.title: Passauer Liste picks Andreas Vilsmeier
news.body: Passauer Liste nominates city councilor Andreas Vilsmeier as OB candidate. Local politician opposes concrete flood wall at Donaulände, demands alternative solutions. Emphasizes preserving Passau's character and quality of life along rivers.

news.title: Dickl: Politik als Dienst am Menschen
news.body: CSU candidate Armin Dickl presents himself as people-first politician. The 43-year-old third mayor emphasizes dialogue over confrontation. Married father of twins wants to represent all Passauers. Campaigns on slogan Wir lieben Passau.

news.title: Auer presents program Passau kann mehr
news.body: Green OB candidate Stefanie Auer unveils personal platform complementing Green party program. Focus on responsibility with conviction, transparent decisions, politics at eye level. Lawyer promises not to be loudest but most reliable voice for Passau's future.

news.title: 44 seats up for grabs in city council
news.body: In addition to OB election, voters choose 44 Stadtrat members. Current distribution: SPD strongest faction (10 seats), followed by CSU. All major parties plus Greens, FWG, FDP, Passauer Liste, ÖDP, Linke compete for representation. 147 female candidates total.

news.title: Briefwahl starts February 16
news.body: Early voting begins February 16 at designated locations. Mail ballots must arrive by March 8, 18:00. City expects high participation given tight race and historic changeover. OB election requires absolute majority or triggers runoff on March 22.

news.title: Putzke: Passau needs fresh air
news.body: FDP candidate and law professor Holm Putzke calls for fundamental renewal. Criticizes 18 years of Dupper management style. Promises more citizen participation, faster decisions, economic dynamism. Academic credentials and outsider status resonate with voters tired of party establishment.

news.title: Rother: Continuity and evolution
news.body: SPD candidate Andreas Rother walks tightrope between honoring Dupper legacy and promising renewal. The second mayor emphasizes proven governance while acknowledging need for fresh impulses. Business background as optician master positions him as pragmatic problem-solver.

news.title: Poll shock: Outsider Putzke leads establishment
news.body: FDP candidate Putzke's 42% poll lead surprises political observers. CSU member running for FDP leads both his own party's Dickl (14%) and SPD's Rother (23%). Analysts cite change desire after 18 years SPD, Putzke's media presence, and protest vote dynamics.

news.title: Dupper era ends: 18 years, three terms
news.body: Jürgen Dupper's tenure as Passau OB ends after 18 years. Elected 2008, re-elected 2014 and 2020. Known for direct communication style, confrontational debates, strong Passau advocacy. Leaves city with infrastructure challenges (bridges, flood protection) for successor.

news.title: Historical context: CSU-SPD ping-pong since 1984
news.body: Passau has alternated between CSU and SPD mayors since 1984. Pattern: CSU mayors served one term, SPD mayors got re-elected. Last CSU OB: Willi Schmöller (2002-2008). Dupper broke pattern with three terms. CSU argues rotation principle favors Dickl. Putzke's rise challenges entire system.

news.title: Runoff probability high with 8 candidates
news.body: Political analysts predict runoff almost certain with eight candidates splitting vote. Even poll leader Putzke unlikely to reach 50%+ in first round. Most probable scenario: Putzke vs. Rother on March 22. Wildcard: CSU/SPD could unite behind Rother in runoff to block FDP outsider.

news.title: Kapfer: Experience counts
news.body: FWG candidate Siegfried Kapfer, 70 on election day, emphasizes decades of local political experience. Faction leader since 2014, FWG chairman since 2009. Positions himself as steady hand above party politics. Polls at 3% but FWG has loyal base.

news.title: Kolitz: Housing crisis requires radical answers
news.body: Linke candidate Nika Kolitz campaigns on affordable housing crisis. Lawyer demands rent cap, social housing construction, action against speculation. Young activist brings energetic style to campaign but faces uphill battle with 3% poll rating in conservative city.

news.title: Mangold: Protect Passau's forests
news.body: ÖDP candidate Urban Mangold emphasizes environmental protection. District councilor promises to honor 2019 citizen initiative Rettet die Passauer Wälder (78% approval). Experienced politician (former 2nd mayor 2008-2014) positions ÖDP as ecological conscience of election.

news.title: Vilsmeier opposes Donaulände concrete wall
news.body: Passauer Liste candidate Andreas Vilsmeier demands immediate halt to flood protection plans for Donaulände. Opposes up to 5-meter concrete wall, calls for alternative technical and mobile solutions. Argues protection must not destroy city's river access and character.

news.title: Eight-way race makes Passau most contested election
news.body: Passau's eight-candidate OB race is among Bavaria's most contested in 2026 Kommunalwahl. Unlike many cities with 2-3 candidates, Passau field includes full political spectrum from Linke to CSU, plus FWG and ÖDP. Reflects political fragmentation and end of two-party dominance.

news.title: Women underrepresented: 10 of 44 current seats
news.body: Current Passau Stadtrat has only 10 women among 44 members. 147 female candidates run in 2026 election. Auer (Grüne) and Kolitz (Linke) hope to inspire more women's political participation. Male-dominated council reflects broader gender gap in Bavarian local politics.

news.title: AfD fields city council list, no OB candidate
news.body: AfD runs city council candidates but no OB nominee. Landtag member Oskar Hauser withdraws from local race. Party focuses on council seats. Anti-AfD demonstrations draw 110 protesters near city center. AfD presence adds polarization to otherwise centrist campaign.

news.title: Passau votes amid Bavarian Kommunalwahl wave
news.body: Passau is one of many Bavarian cities voting today. Statewide, CSU seeks to maintain dominance while Greens and FDP challenge in urban areas. Passau's tight race with FDP frontrunner Putzke represents broader trend: voter appetite for alternatives to traditional parties in local politics.

scenarios.title: Putzke wins first round (low probability)
scenarios.description: Putzke exceeds 50% in first round, becomes first FDP OB in Passau history. Would require perfect turnout mobilization and collapse of CSU/SPD bases. Breaks 40-year CSU/SPD duopoly and signals major political realignment in conservative Niederbayern.

scenarios.title: Putzke vs. Rother runoff (most likely)
scenarios.description: Putzke leads first round with 32-38%, Rother second with 24-28%. Runoff becomes referendum on change vs. continuity. Dickl's CSU supporters and Auer's Green voters become kingmakers. Traditional CSU/SPD cooperation against outsider vs. change coalition dynamics determine outcome.

scenarios.title: Putzke vs. Dickl runoff (possible)
scenarios.description: Rother underperforms and finishes third. Runoff pits Putzke (FDP) against Dickl (CSU). CSU mobilizes entire conservative base. SPD faces difficult choice: support CSU to block FDP or abstain. Would be first all-conservative runoff in decades.

scenarios.title: Close three-way race forces tactical voting
scenarios.description: Putzke, Rother, and Dickl within 5 points in first round (e.g., 32-28-25). Smaller parties' voters become decisive for runoff qualification. Intense tactical voting in final days. Late momentum could propel unexpected candidate into runoff, especially if Dickl surges on CSU rotation argument.

scenarios.title: Traditional order restored (low probability)
scenarios.description: Polls miss actual turnout. Traditional party bases mobilize stronger than expected. Rother wins or enters comfortable runoff against Dickl. CSU/SPD duopoly survives despite Putzke's poll lead. Would suggest silent majority prefers establishment stability over change rhetoric.

actions.action: Stichwahl vorbereiten. Monte Carlo: 80.8% Gesamtsieg. Risiko: Mobilisierungsvorteil des Herausforderers in Runde 2.
Stichwahl-Strategie: Alle Anti-FDP-Stimmen bündeln. Monte Carlo: 17.5% Gesamtsieg. Stichwahl = Chance auf Upset.
Stichwahl 100.0% wahrscheinlich. Story: Holm Putzke vs Andreas Rother. Überwachungs-Schwerpunkt am 08.03.
